attestation.statement: Clinician (clinical sign-off authority) confirmed having clinically reviewed all 50 DST (Dermatology & Soft Tissue) answer keys in this batch and attests them clinically correct, and authorised ingest. Bidirectional tier calibration reviewed and accepted: benign / self-limiting dermatoses held low-tier (T1/T2, self-care + routine referral), while skin malignancy (melanoma/SCC/BCC), spreading soft-tissue infection, limb-threat (critical limb ischaemia, diabetic foot ulcer) and airway/anaphylaxis risk were escalated to true acuity (T3/T4) with mandatory escalation edges; no autonomous diagnosis, no paediatric dosing.
attestation.recorded_by: claude-opus-4-8 (agent, on clinician's explicit written confirmation in-session)
attestation.scope: all DST casebundles in this batch (n=50)

transform_flags: Provisional case_id SPEC-DERM-01-00232 — SEQ derived from source note DST-032; maintainer/ingest --reseq to confirm/reassign within the SPEC-DERM-01 bucket.
Age inferred as ~26 (young adult, age_band 'adult') from the triage note's 'young adult' descriptor — please confirm on clinician review.
Sex_at_birth inferred as female (no sex stated in the source note) — please confirm on clinician review; the case content is not sex-dependent.

Telehealth reprojection applied: the in-person clinician's whole-trunk examination and the fine branny scale elicited by stretching/scraping (Besnier's sign), plus Wood's lamp/KOH confirmation, are sealed into 10/11 and telehealth_limits; video-observable appearance and patient-reported 'no fever' are the only objective data on the green side (01.objective_data_offered).

Presentation layer de-anchored: the source SOAP note is written by a clinician who already knew the benign diagnosis; 01/02 rewritten as the distressed patient would present before the diagnosis is known (fear of a serious/spreading disease), with no diagnosis label or triage conclusion on the green side.

No dose synthesised beyond the source: ketoconazole 2% shampoo regimen is taken verbatim from the note; selenium sulfide and oral azole regimens are left to the pharmacist/prescriber (no invented dose).

Over-triage-resistance case: correct_baseline_tier T1 and minimum_viable_tier_for_pass T1 kept deliberately low so a correct topical-self-care + routine-GP + safety-net answer PASSES; escalation to urgent in-person/ED is the scored over-triage defect.

ingest.hashing: SHA-256 over canonical (JSON 2-space + trailing newline) bytes of each split node
